shotsDetail.outcome: Off Target - Player On Ball Error

players.name: Kevin DE BRUYNE
players.highlights: Orchestrated Belgium's attack with 59 offers made and 4 shots on goal; forced constant defensive adjustments in the Egyptian midfield.

players.name: Leandro TROSSARD
players.highlights: Constant creative threat — 3 shots including the 38' deflected effort that produced Belgium's only goal; 9 take-ons and strong progressive movement.

players.name: Nathan NGOY
players.highlights: Most industrious defender — 70 passes at 97% completion, 13 line breaks completed, and team-high top speed of 34.5 km/h.

players.name: EMAM ASHOUR
players.highlights: Scored Egypt's opener at 20' with an incisive right-foot finish; led the team with 6 ball progressions and 4 take-ons before being substituted.

players.name: OMAR MARMOUSH
players.highlights: Egypt's most dangerous player — 5 attempts at goal, 4 ball progressions, 3 take-ons; constant menace in behind Belgium's defensive line.

players.name: MOHANAD LASHIN
players.highlights: Defensive engine of Egypt's compact block — 17 tackles made (most in the match), 16 direct pressures, anchored EGY's aggressive midfield press.

BEL.summary: Belgium dominated possession (45.5%) and created more chances (15 shots, xG 1.07) but were frustrated by Egypt's deep block. Their only goal came from a fortunate 38' deflection. De Bruyne pulled strings in the #10 role but lacked the clinical finishing to beat an inspired Shoubir.
strengths: Sustained territorial dominance with 181 receptions in the final third
De Bruyne's creativity and movement (59 offers, 4 shots)
Nathan NGOY's composure and range of passing from CB (70 passes, 97%)
Effective wide threat through Doku's take-ons (9) and Meunier's overlapping runs
weaknesses: Lack of clinical finishing — only 4 shots on target from 15 attempts
Struggled to break down Egypt's low block (37% of EGY's OOP in low block)
Set-piece delivery was limited (2 corners, only 1 outswing delivery)
Defensive transitions exposed at times against Egypt's counter-press

EGY.summary: Egypt executed a disciplined defensive game plan, sitting in a low block (37%) and punishing Belgium on the counter. EMAM ASHOUR's early goal gave them a platform to defend, while MOHANAD LASHIN's midfield dominance (17 tackles) and OMAR MARMOUSH's individual quality kept Belgium's defence uncomfortable.
strengths: Organized defensive block — 59 tackles, 52 forced turnovers, 258 pressures
MOHANAD LASHIN's defensive dominance anchoring the midfield (17 tackles, 16 direct pressures)
Clinical counter-attacking — EMAM ASHOUR's goal from Egypt's limited open-play opportunities (xG 0.73 from 14 shots)
Goalkeeper SHOUBIR's 100% save percentage from 17 attempts faced
weaknesses: Low possession share (38.1%) limited sustained attacking play
Only 13 open-play crosses completed and poor cross conversion
Substitution management disrupted momentum — 5 subs in final 19 minutes
Vulnerability to Belgium's movement off the ball (358 offers made by BEL)
